body_excerpt:
Greptile Summary

This PR introduces first-class keyspace notification support for StackExchange.Redis, including new `KeyNotification` / `KeyNotificationType` / `KeyNotificationTypeFastHash` types, factory methods on `RedisChannel` for building well-typed keyspace and keyevent subscription channels, and a refactored `Subscription` hierarchy that adds a `MultiNodeSubscription` class for broadcasting subscriptions across all cluster nodes. - **New keyspace API**: `RedisChannel.KeyEvent`, `KeySpacePrefix`, `KeySpacePattern`, `KeySpaceSingleKey` build correctly-formed subscription channels; `KeyNotification` exposes alloc-free key/type access; `ChannelMessage.TryParseKeyNotification` provides a convenient entry point. - **Subscription refactor**: The single `Subscription` class is split into `SingleNodeSubscription` (unchanged semantics) and `MultiNodeSubscription` (subscribes/unsubscribes across all primaries), with `ResultProcessor` and `ServerSelectionStrategy` updated to wire up multi-node tracking. - **Channel prefix handling**: `RawResult.AsRedisChannel` and `PhysicalConnection.Write` are updated so that keyspace channels bypass the user-configured channel prefix, preventing missed notifications when a prefix is active.

Confidence Score: 2/5

Not safe to merge as-is: the inverted condition in MultiNodeSubscription silently destroys active keyspace subscriptions on every reconnect cycle. The new MultiNodeSubscription class is the heart of the keyspace notification feature, and its RemoveDisconnectedEndpoints method removes connected servers rather than disconnected ones. Every reconnection event (network blip, server restart, heartbeat-triggered UpdateSubscriptions) would evict all active server associations, causing the subscription to stop delivering messages without any error signal to the caller. src/StackExchange.Redis/Subscription.cs — the MultiNodeSubscription.RemoveDisconnectedEndpoints method needs the condition inverted; src/Stack
